# One-Day Marathon Training Meal Plan

**Athlete Profile:** 180cm, 72kg | Goal: Marathon training - High carbohydrate endurance nutrition

---

## BREAKFAST (7:00 AM)
**Oatmeal with Banana and Honey**
- 80g rolled oats
- 300ml whole milk
- 1 medium banana (120g)
- 2 tbsp honey (40g)
- 1 tbsp almond butter (16g)
- Pinch of salt

**Nutritional targets:** 620 cal | 18g protein | 102g carbs | 11g fat

---

## MID-MORNING SNACK (10:00 AM)
**Pre-Run Energy Boost**
- 2 slices whole wheat bread (60g)
- 30g peanut butter
- 1 apple (180g)
- Sports drink (500ml, 6% carbs)

**Nutritional targets:** 520 cal | 14g protein | 68g carbs | 16g fat

---

## LUNCH (1:00 PM)
**Chicken & Rice Bowl**
- 150g grilled chicken breast
- 200g cooked white rice
- 100g steamed broccoli
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- Mixed green salad with lemon dressing

**Nutritional targets:** 750 cal | 42g protein | 92g carbs | 12g fat

---

## PRE-TRAINING SNACK (3:00 PM - 2 hours before run)
**Quick Carbohydrates**
- 2 rice cakes (30g)
- 40g honey or jam
- 200ml orange juice
- 1 tbsp almond butter (16g)

**Nutritional targets:** 420 cal | 8g protein | 78g carbs | 8g fat

---

## DINNER (7:00 PM - Post-Run Recovery)
**Salmon with Sweet Potato & Vegetables**
- 180g grilled salmon fillet
- 250g baked sweet potato
- 120g roasted carrots and zucchini
- 2 tbsp olive oil for cooking
- Sea salt and black pepper

**Nutritional targets:** 780 cal | 48g protein | 76g carbs | 18g fat

---

## EVENING RECOVERY DRINK (Before bed)
**Chocolate Milk Recovery Shake**
- 400ml whole milk
- 30g chocolate powder
- 1 tbsp honey (20g)
- ½ banana (60g)

**Nutritional targets:** 340 cal | 14g protein | 58g carbs | 8g fat

---

## DAILY TOTALS
| Metric | Amount |
|--------|--------|
| **Calories** | ~3,410 |
| **Protein** | 144g (16.9% of calories) |
| **Carbohydrates** | 474g (55.5% of calories) |
| **Fat** | 73g (19.3% of calories) |
| **Fiber** | ~28g |

---

## Key Marathon Training Nutrition Notes

✅ **High Carbohydrate Loading:** 474g carbs (6.6g/kg) supports glycogen storage for endurance performance
✅ **Adequate Protein:** 144g (2.0g/kg) supports muscle recovery and repair from long-distance training
✅ **Timing Strategy:** 
   - Pre-run snack 2 hours before with easily digestible carbs
   - Recovery meal within 30-60 minutes post-run with protein + carbs
   - Evening milk drink aids overnight muscle recovery

✅ **Hydration:** Aim for 3-4 liters water daily + electrolytes during runs exceeding 90 minutes
✅ **Electrolytes:** Include sports drinks on long training days (>75 minutes)
✅ **Budget-Friendly:** Uses economical staples - rice, oats, chicken, eggs, seasonal produce

**Adjustments:**
- **Long run days (>20km):** Increase calories to 3,800+ kcal
